item.title: How to Plant and Grow Fruiting Pear Trees
item.description:
Dreaming of a home orchard? Fruiting pear trees are a great choice. They aren’t troubled by most diseases or pests, and deliver bumper crops of delicious fruit. Reap all these rewards by following this guide that takes you through every step, from planting to harvest and eating or cooking. Read more now.

The post How to Plant and Grow Fruiting Pear Trees appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for Shooting Star Wildflowers
item.description:
Shooting star is an early spring wildflower native to eastern and central North America, where its rarity has earned it protected status in numerous regions. It prefers partially shaded fertile prairies and open woodlands, and is well-suited to Zones 4 to 8. Learn how to grow and care for Primula meadia in this guide.

The post Learn How to Grow and Care for Shooting Star Wildflowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Black Mondo Grass
item.description:
Prized for lush mounds of dark, blade-like foliage, black mondo grass is an easy choice for beds, containers, and ground covers for sunny or shady gardens. Primarily a foliage plant, summer brings delicate mauve flowers followed by blackish berries in fall. Learn how to grow and care for black mondo grass in this guide.

item.title: 39 Common Weeds That You Can Eat or Use for Medicine

Weeds can be wonderful. Don’t believe me? One bite of chickweed salad and you’ll probably be begging the notorious weed to visit your garden. item.title: How to Plant and Grow Epazote (Wormseed)
item.description:
Epazote is a pungent, aromatic herb that has been a cornerstone of Mexican and Central American cooking since the time of the Aztecs. Its jagged green leaves have a complex, hard-to-pin-down flavor combining hints of mint, citrus, anise, and oregano. Learn how to plant and grow epazote in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Plant and Grow Epazote (Wormseed) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Avocado rootstocks: What do they matter?
item.description:
Rootstocks are mysterious. They do their work hidden, underground. Yet, their influence on the trees above ground is real and measurable. For example, some rootstocks have demonstrated that they can induce the tops to make more than twice as much fruit compared to other rootstocks. This was first shown back in the 1980s and 1990s […]

The post Avocado rootstocks: What do they matter? appeared first on Greg Alder's Yard Posts: Southern California food gardening.

item.title: CDFA Climate Bond Grants: accepting concept proposals for Healthy Soils Program and State Water Efficiency and Enhancement Program
item.description:
CDFA’s  Office of Agricultural Resilience and Sustainability (OARS) is pleased to announce that Concept Proposal applications are now open for both the State Water Efficiency and Enhancement Program (SWEEP) and the Healthy Soils Program (HSP) Block Grants, funded by the Climate Bond.  This milestone reflects the continued momentum … Continue reading →

The post CDFA Climate Bond Grants: accepting concept proposals for Healthy Soils Program and State Water Efficiency and Enhancement Program appeared first on CDFA's Planting Seeds Blog.
item.source:markdown:

item.title: Learn How to Plant and Grow Shiso Herb (Perilla)
item.description:
Shiso is a fragrant annual herb with frilly, flavorful leaves in shades of green or purple. A staple of Japanese, Korean, and Chinese cuisine, shiso is easy to grow from seed in full sun and well-draining soil. It's a rewarding and versatile addition to any herb garden. Learn how to plant and grow perilla in this guide.

The post Learn How to Plant and Grow Shiso Herb (Perilla) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Shiso is a fragrant annual herb with frilly, flavorful leaves in shades of green or purple. A staple of Japanese, Korean, and Chinese cuisine, shiso is easy to grow from seed in full sun and well-draining soil. It's a rewarding and versatile addition to any herb garden. Learn how to plant and grow perilla in this guide.

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Edelweiss Flowers
item.description:
Edelweiss (Leontopodium nivale) is a beautiful Alpine wildflower, native to the rocky limestone slopes of the European Alps made famous by The Sound of Music. It produces clusters of woolly white star-shaped blooms in summer above silvery felted foliage. Learn how to grow edelweiss flowers in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow and Care for Edelweiss Flowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:

item.title: How to Grow Dutchman’s Breeches (Dicentra)
item.description:
Dutchman's breeches (Dicentra cucullaria) is native to the woodland floors of eastern North America. Its delicate, fern-like foliage and clusters of white pantaloon-shaped blooms emerge in early spring, disappearing by summer as the plant goes dormant. Learn how to grow this charming spring ephemeral in this guide.

The post How to Grow Dutchman’s Breeches (Dicentra) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
